evals.prompt: generate the schema for this RFC: requirements/rfc-review/scripts/fixtures/well_structured_rfc.md
evals.expected_output: schema.ir.json, a rendered schema.sql (or json-schema, or both), and SCHEMA_NOTES.md written next to the RFC and shown inline. Because the RFC is light on DB-level field detail, most fields are marked assumed:true with a stated reason, and if entities genuinely can't be identified the skill says so rather than inventing a model.
assertions: schema.ir.json, a rendered target file, and SCHEMA_NOTES.md are all written next to the RFC and shown inline
Fields not explicitly stated in the RFC are marked assumed: true with an assumed_reason, both in the IR and in the rendered output
The IR uses only the abstract type system, no target-specific syntax like VARCHAR or Prisma types

evals.prompt: derive the data model from this ADR: requirements/rfc-review/scripts/fixtures/adr_style.md
evals.expected_output: A schema.ir.json describing an Event entity with a JSONB-style payload field, rendered to SQL DDL by default (Postgres-flavored) unless the user is asked to choose a target because none was detectable.
assertions: An Event entity (or equivalently named) with a payload field is present in the IR
If the target format was ambiguous, the skill asks via AskUserQuestion instead of silently defaulting
